name: Asteria
description: Federal parliamentary republic on the eastern side of the Varethian continent, formed in 1847 from fragmented princedoms. Bounded by the Caldon Mountains to the west and the Vythian Sea to the east.

attributes.government: Federal parliamentary republic with ceremonial presidency

name: Velden
description: Neighbouring country to the south of Asteria, sharing the border along the Southern Marches. Relations have been marked by periodic disputes over frontier territory.

name: Caldon Autonomous District
description: Disputed administrative district established in 1953 within the Western Uplands, intended to grant self‑governance to Caldon communities. Its borders have been contested since inception, with a boycotted referendum in 1963.

name: Asterhaven
description: Capital and largest city of Asteria, located on the Eastern Coast at a deep natural harbour. Originally the seat of the Althar dynasty, it was renamed in 1874 after the monarchy was overthrown.

name: Marlow
description: Coastal city in the Eastern Coast region, known for its deep natural harbour and shipbuilding industry. A secondary port after Asterhaven, with a strong fishing fleet.

name: Port Selene
description: Historic port on the Eastern Coast, originally an independent city‑state until annexed by the Kingdom of Althar in 1809. Still retains a distinct maritime culture and minor independence sentiment.

name: Sylene River
description: Major river flowing eastward from the Caldon Mountains, bisecting Asteria and emptying into the Vythian Sea through a marshy delta. The fertile Sylene Valley depends on its waters for intensive agriculture.

name: Mount Calmor
description: Prominent peak in the Caldon Mountains, rising in the Western Uplands. Known for its distinctive double summit and frequent cloud cover; a source of marble quarried since the early 19th century.

name: Sylene Valley
description: The fertile central lowlands flanking the Sylene River, forming the agricultural and demographic core of Asteria. Dense population, intensive farming, and major cities characterize this region.

name: Southern Marches
description: Arid plains and limestone hills in southern Asteria, known for sheep farming and wine production. Historically a frontier with sparse population and periodic border disputes.

name: Bromby
description: A small market town in the eastern Sylene Valley, serving as a local hub for grain trade and milling. Established in the mid-18th century along an old trade road.

name: Colden
description: A mining settlement in the Northern Highlands, founded during the iron boom of the 1860s. Known for its harsh winters and tight-knit Caldon community.

name: Calbrook
description: A swift tributary of the Sylene River, originating high in the Caldon Mountains and joining the main river near the town of Bridgeford. Important for irrigation in the Western Uplands.

name: Tarn River
description: An intermittent river in the Southern Marches, flowing only during the spring rains. Its dry bed serves as a natural boundary for sheep grazing claims.

name: Mount Durnik
description: A prominent granite peak in the eastern Caldon Mountains, visible from the Caldon Autonomous District. Named after a legendary Caldon hunter.

name: Storm Peak
description: The highest summit in the Northern Highlands, known for sudden weather changes and frequent lightning strikes. It marks the traditional boundary between lowland and highland territories.

name: Northern Highlands
description: Cool, forested uplands with small mining settlements; strong Caldon cultural influence; sparse population; known for herding and timber.

name: Caldon Mountains
description: Rugged mountain range defining the western border of Asteria, reaching up to 2,800 m. Rich in iron and copper, with scattered Caldon mining towns.

name: University of Port Selene
description: A public research university founded in 1875 in Port Selene, focusing on maritime studies and trade law. Its library holds the largest collection of nautical charts in Asteria.

name: The Caldon Monthly
description: A bilingual Asterian–Caldon newspaper established in 1903 in Colden, reporting on highlands affairs and cultural events. It is distributed across the Caldon Mountains and Northern Highlands.

name: Asterian Reform League
description: A centrist political party founded in 1935 advocating for electoral reform and decentralisation. It has held the chancellery in coalition governments from 1948 to 1962.

name: Southern Marches Winegrowers' Association
description: An agricultural cooperative founded in 1912 to promote wine production and quality standards in the Southern Marches. It runs annual tastings and maintains regional appellation rules.

name: National Archives of Asteria
description: The federal repository for government and historical records, founded in 1850 in Asterhaven. It suffered damage during the 1933 economic crisis but was restored in 1936.

name: Bromby Mining Company
description: A mining firm founded in 1867 that extracts iron ore from the Caldon Mountains foothills near Bromby. It supplies a third of Asteria's domestic iron.

name: The Sylene Valley Herald
description: A daily newspaper established in 1838 in Sylene Valley, covering agriculture, politics, and commerce. It is the most widely read paper in the lowlands.

name: Caldon Front for Autonomy
description: A regional political party founded in 1946 that campaigns for increased self‑governance of the Caldon Autonomous District and recognition of Caldon language rights.

name: University of the Western Uplands
description: A regional university serving the Western Uplands, with emphasis on agricultural and environmental sciences.

name: Althar Railway Company
description: Operated the country's first rail line connecting the Sylene Valley to the coast; nationalized in 1936.

name: The Eastern Coast Reporter
description: A daily newspaper covering maritime trade and coastal affairs.

name: National Unity Party
description: A centrist party advocating for national cohesion and economic modernization.

name: Federal Bureau of Land Management
description: Responsible for managing public lands and resolving disputes over resource use.

name: Supreme Court of Administrative Appeals
description: The highest court for administrative law cases.

name: Varethian Shipping Line
description: Operated cargo and passenger routes across the Vythian Sea.

name: Althar Institute of Technology
description: A technical university focused on mining engineering and metallurgy.

name: Sylene Valley Agricultural Board
description: Government body overseeing agricultural policy, subsidies, and land management in the Sylene Valley. Established during the post-war reconstruction period to improve food security and address rural disparities.

name: Caldon Ironworks
description: Historic iron and steel producer in the Caldon Mountains. Its furnaces were central to Asteria's industrialization and railway expansion during the 19th century.

name: Kira Durnik
description: Caldon clan leader during the late pre-Unification period, known for resisting encroachment by the Kingdom of Althar.

name: Maren Vasilov
description: Delegate at the Unification Convention of 1847 and later served as the first Chancellor under the monarchy.

name: Tarven Thalar
description: Leader of the populist revolution that established the First Republic; served as President from 1921 to 1928.

name: Lira Borok
description: Prominent Caldon poet and linguist, instrumental in the recognition of Caldon as a regional language in 1953.

name: Velden Morsen
description: Mining engineer who modernized copper extraction in the Caldon Mountains; founder of the Caldon Ironworks.

name: Sora Thalassen
description: Novelist and journalist; chronicled rural life in the Sylene Valley and criticized the failed land reform of 1928.

name: Durnik Calmork
description: Leader of the Caldon Mountain clans during the 1809 uprising against the forced absorption of Port Selene.

name: Maren Thalarsen
description: Chancellor of Asteria from 1978 to 1990, known for economic reforms and efforts to address regional disparities.

name: Lina Vaskov
description: A prominent Asterian politician from the Sylene Valley, known for her work in land reform during the Federal Era.

name: Riven Sorbek
description: Caldon poet and activist from the Caldon Mountains, whose works in the Caldon language advocated for linguistic preservation.

name: Vela Lenar
description: Asterian merchant and shipowner from Asterhaven, instrumental in establishing the first steamship line on the Sylene River.

name: Harken Kovalen
description: Caldon politician and leader of the Caldon Front for Autonomy, a key figure in the autonomy movement after 1953.

name: Selene Serkov
description: Journalist and women's suffrage activist from Port Selene, founder of the weekly newspaper 'The Selene Independent'.

name: Kaelen Tarnov
description: Federal politician from the Southern Marches, a leading voice for rural development and water rights in the 1990s.

name: Lorna Varen
description: Botanist from the Northern Highlands who catalogued the endemic alpine flora of Mount Calmor and the Caldon range.

name: Tarven Marok
description: Caldon clan chieftain from the Caldon Mountains who led a rebellion against the Kingdom of Althar during the Pre-Unification era.

name: Elara Durnov
description: A poet from the late Unified era, known for romanticizing the Sylene Valley in works such as 'Harvest Moon Echoes'.

name: Joren Kalenar
description: First Chancellor from the Caldon community, serving from 1960 to 1968 and advocating for regional autonomy within the federal system.

name: Mira Darsen
description: A botanist who catalogued the flora of the Caldon Mountains, contributing to the University of Port Selene's herbarium.

name: Falden Norsov
description: Founder of the Sylene Valley Steel Company, instrumental in Asteria's industrialization during the late 19th century.

name: Tova Vardov
description: A Caldon folk singer who traveled the Northern Highlands collecting oral epics and preserving traditional mountain music.

name: Arven Thorsen
description: A Supreme Court justice who presided over the landmark Caldon Autonomy Case of 1955, which clarified regional powers.

name: Lena Kalenar
description: A leader of the suffrage movement in Asteria, organizing demonstrations in Asterhaven during the First Republic.

name: Doren Selenov
description: An explorer and cartographer who mapped the upper Sylene River's tributaries in the 1890s, producing the first detailed charts of the region.

name: Tara Korak
description: Historian specializing in the First Republic period; authored 'The Broken Decade' (1949).

name: Alden Solov
description: First Chancellor of the Federal Era (1934–1940), oversaw the drafting of the new constitution.

name: Borin Durnok
description: Leading figure in the Caldon linguistic revival; founded the Caldon Cultural Council in 1953.

name: Kara Barek
description: Poet known for blending Sylene Creole with formal Asterian; her collection 'River Songs' (1935) was controversial.

name: Carven Thalik
description: Founder of the Caldon Ironworks in 1875, a major mining and metallurgy enterprise in the Caldon Mountains.

name: Eda Tarnik
description: Surveyor and cartographer; her 1882 expedition determined the source of the Sylene River.

attributes.notable_achievement: mapped the source of the Sylene River

name: Sylene Creole Merchants
description: A social and economic group of traders in the Sylene Valley and along the Eastern Coast who use Sylene Creole as a lingua franca. They emerged during the late 19th century as river and coastal trade expanded.

name: Caldon Mountain Clans
description: Kinship-based social units among the Caldon-speaking highlanders. Each clan claims descent from a legendary mountain founder. Clan identity remains strong despite federal integration.

name: Sylene Valley Irrigation Network
description: A system of canals and channels diverting water from the Sylene River to agricultural fields in the Sylene Valley. Originally built in the late 19th century, it was expanded during the Federal Era.

name: Caldon Copper Forging
description: Traditional metalworking technique used by Caldon smiths to create tools, jewelry, and decorative items from locally mined copper. Distinctive patterns and hardening methods are passed down through apprenticeships.

name: Asteria Rising: The 1934 Revision
description: The official 1934 rewritten version of the national anthem, commissioned by the Federal Assembly to replace the original 1848 monarchist lyrics. The book includes the new score and commentary on the changes.

name: The Unification Tapestry
description: A large woven tapestry measuring 12 by 6 meters, created in 1850 to commemorate the Unification Convention of 1847. It depicts delegates from all signatory states in the Assembly Hall of Asterhaven.

name: Principles of Federal Governance
description: A comprehensive treatise on Asteria's federal parliamentary system, written by political theorist Torven Norsen and published in 1950. It is widely used in university curricula and by civil servants.

name: Portrait of the Last King
description: An oil painting of King Orven III, the last monarch of the Althar dynasty, completed in 1919. It hangs in the Asterhaven City Museum and is noted for its somber tone.

name: Sylene River Folklore
description: A compiled collection of folk stories and river legends from the Sylene Valley, first published in 1889 in Asterhaven. The book incorporates both Asterian and Sylene Creole oral traditions.

name: Borvath
description: A unitary semi-presidential republic south of Asteria's Southern Marches, separated by the Tarn River. It consists of fertile plains and river valleys, with a powerful executive. The capital is Tarnsport, and a notable town is Durnik's Crossing. Borvath's economy relies on grain and wine, and it has a contested border with Asteria over Tarn River islands.

name: Lyssar
description: A federal theocracy in the northern highlands beyond the Calbrook River, ruled by the Council of Seers. It is a cold region of forested hills and lakes. The capital is Lakehaven, and a secondary city is Coldwater. Lyssar has historically claimed parts of the Northern Highlands and maintains a tense relationship with Asteria over religious influence.

name: Kalden
description: Capital and largest city of Velden, situated in a high valley of the Caldon Mountains. Known for its royal palace and silver market.

name: Thornwood
description: A mining town in northern Velden, famous for its silver mines and proximity to the border with Asteria.

name: Tarnsport
description: Capital of Borvath, located at a major crossing on the Tarn River. It is a hub for grain trade and river transport.

name: Durnik's Crossing
description: A smaller town in Borvath near the border with Asteria, named after the nearby Durnik family. It serves as a border crossing and market.

name: Lakehaven
description: Capital of Lyssar, situated on the shores of a large glacial lake. It houses the Council of Seers and is a religious center.

name: Coldwater
description: A fortified town in eastern Lyssar, near the border with Asteria's Northern Highlands. Known for its cold springs and defensive walls.

name: King Arven Thorsen
description: King of Velden from 1850 to 1880, known for modernizing the Veldeni military and infrastructure, and for negotiating the Treaty of Colden after the Caldon Mountain War.

name: Royal Academy of Metallurgy
description: An institute in Velden dedicated to advancing metallurgy and mining techniques, especially silver extraction. Its graduates often work in the Caldon Mountains mines.

name: University of Tarnsport
description: The leading university in Borvath, known for its agricultural and hydrological studies, especially concerning the Tarn River.

name: The Order of the Silent Flame
description: A religious order in Lyssar that advises the Council of Seers. Its members, known as Silent Monks, practice meditation and record history. The order is deeply connected to Lyssar's theocratic governance.
